text.title: Judge Calibration Protocol Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure from label scope to a shipped calibrated judge.

step.name: scope-labels
step.action: Enumerate the labels the judge must produce. Binary (refused/complied) is preferred; ordinal (1-5 rubric) requires more cases. Define what each label means with two reference examples per label.
step.decision-gate: If >5 labels, split into multiple binary judges; nested categorical scoring is unreliable.

step.name: build-holdout
step.action: Stratified sample 30-50 cases ensuring ≥30% minority class. Hand-label by ≥1 rater (2 for safety-critical). Track inter-rater disagreement.
step.decision-gate: If inter-rater κ < 0.7, label policy is unclear — fix the spec before proceeding.

step.name: run-judge
step.action: Run judge over the holdout once. Record judge_label per case. Hash the judge prompt for traceability.
step.decision-gate: Sanity-check: judge produced a label for every case; no parse errors.

step.name: compute-metrics
step.action: Compute confusion matrix, accuracy, Cohen's κ, false-pass rate, false-fail rate. Use sklearn or the included helper.
step.decision-gate: If κ ≥ 0.7 and per-class precision ≥ 0.8 → continue; else step 5.

step.name: diagnose
step.action: Cluster the disagreement cases. If one cluster dominates, revise the judge prompt to handle it (add a counter-example). If disagreements are diffuse, revise the label spec. Track which path was taken.
step.output: revised judge-prompt.md OR revised label-spec.md + record-of-changes.
step.decision-gate: Re-run from step 3 (max 3 iterations); after 3 iterations escalate to a different judge model.

step.name: ship-or-block
step.action: If κ ≥ 0.7 AND accuracy ≥ 0.85 AND minority-class recall ≥ 0.75 → decision=ship. Else decision=revise_prompt / revise_holdout / block per the failure path.
step.output: Final report + judge prompt + holdout pinned to commit.
step.decision-gate: "ship" decision is reversible — re-calibrate on next prompt or model change.
